utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All five items appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all details requested: the format asks for each category 'with costs' and for total budget 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown.' - health_coverage: Includes description of physical and mental health insurance and provides a cost (€165/month). Fully specified. - property_coverage: States home/contents insurance with costs; provided plan covers equipment and liability, has a cost (€130/month). It does not explicitly say 'home/contents,' but it is property coverage with costs and details. Accept as specified. - travel_coverage: Includes travel insurance details and a cost (€50/month). Fully specified. - additional_services: Provides enhancement service details and cost indication ('Included in property coverage cost'). This counts as a cost specification. Fully specified. - total_budget: Provides monthly (€345) and annual (€4,140) costs. Fully specified. Therefore, 5/5 items are included and fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We need to map each item named in the final coverage plan to the exact corresponding item name in the ground-truth ratings and extract its rating number. The final plan includes: LifeFirst Standard Plan (as health coverage), PrimeHome Premium Coverage with Technology Protection Add-on (as property coverage), InsurePlus Standard Plan (as travel coverage), and Technology Protection Add-on (as an additional service). In the ground truth: LifeFirst Standard Plan appears under primary_health_coverage.physical_health with rating 9. PrimeHome Premium Coverage appears under property_protection.home_content with rating 3. InsurePlus Standard Plan appears under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance with rating 10. Technology Protection is listed under property_enhancement_services as Technology Protection with rating 8. We will map these exact items to their ratings.
